title: Scope IAM to one function's needs
description: Give each function its own execution role with only the permissions that handler uses, never a shared wildcard role.
body: Use when a function accesses cloud resources (tables, queues, buckets, secrets). Do not attach a broad shared role or wildcard actions/resources, and do not reuse one admin role across functions. Actions: create a dedicated execution role per function, grant only the specific actions on the specific resource ARNs it touches, and add deny-by-default. Validation signals include an IAM policy with no wildcards in action or resource and the function still passing its tests. Risk boundary: an over-permissioned function becomes a lateral-movement foothold if compromised; wildcard permissions on a function are a security finding requiring tightening.
